title: [ExportVerilog] Reference to i0 port when loading struct with i0 field
body:
```mlir
  hw.module @hw_struct_create_in_ui0_ui32_out_struct_address_ui0_data_ui32(%in0: i0, %in0_valid: i1, %in1: i32, %in1_valid: i1, %out0_ready: i1) -> (out0: !hw.struct<address: i0, data: i32>, out0_valid: i1, in0_ready: i1, in1_ready: i1) {
    %0 = comb.and %in0_valid, %in1_valid : i1
    %1 = sv.wire  {hw.verilogName = "_GEN"} : !hw.inout<i1>
    sv.assign %1, %0 : i1
    %2 = sv.read_inout %1 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %3 = comb.and %out0_ready, %2 : i1
    %4 = sv.wire  {hw.verilogName = "_GEN_0"} : !hw.inout<i1>
    sv.assign %4, %3 : i1
    %5 = hw.struct_create (%in0, %in1) : !hw.struct<address: i0, data: i32>
    %6 = sv.read_inout %1 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %7 = sv.read_inout %4 : !hw.inout<i1>
    %8 = sv.read_inout %4 : !hw.inout<i1>
    hw.output %5, %6, %8, %7 : !hw.struct<address: i0, data: i32>, i1, i1, i1
  }
```

```verilog
module hw_struct_create_in_ui0_ui32_out_struct_address_ui0_data_ui32(
  // input  /*Zero Width*/                                                    in0,
     input                                                                    in0_valid,
     input  [31:0]                                                            in1,
     input                                                                    in1_valid,
                                                                              out0_ready,
     output struct packed {logic /*Zero Width*/ address; logic [31:0] data; } out0,
     output                                                                   out0_valid,
                                                                              in0_ready,
                                                                              in1_ready);

  wire _GEN = in0_valid & in1_valid;
  wire _GEN_0 = out0_ready & _GEN;
  assign out0 = '{address: in0, data: in1};
  assign out0_valid = _GEN;
  assign in0_ready = _GEN_0;
  assign in1_ready = _GEN_0;
endmodule
```

`** Error: PyCDESystem/hw_struct_create_in_ui0_ui32_out_struct_address_ui0_data_ui32.sv(15): (vlog-2730) Undefined variable: 'in0'.`

Welp, we apparently do have to worry about zero-bit fields in structs. The fact that the zero bit field gets emitted as a single bit field isn't the problem syntactically in this case, it's that `in0` doesn't exist... but if a generated struct is wider than circt thinks it is, that's gonna be a problem.
